$comment: Source of truth for the apodictic:voice_fingerprint block (Cross-Manuscript Author Voice/Craft Fingerprint). One block per work the author collects under a designated author-root, persisting how that work sits in the author's stylometric space so the module can surface movement across a career — drift, range/growth, unconscious self-imitation, signature tics. It does NO new stylometry: it CONSUMES the existing single-voice AI-prose machinery (SETEC voice_profile / voice_distance + the personal-baseline z-scores) and adds only the persistence-and-longitudinal-diagnosis layer. `register` is the comparability class; drift is compared ONLY within a register (validator F3), per the AI-prose domain-shift caution. `source` names which consumed surface the fingerprint draws on (ai-prose-baseline | voice-distance | pov-voice-profile — the last only for the protagonist-collapse sub-diagnostic). `centroid_ref` points into the consumed audit's output (provenance, presence-checked by F2 — the external value cannot be re-resolved here; the module consumes, it does not compute). `metrics` is a flat map of scalar values (the subset engine types the container as object; per-value scalar checks are in Python, the retcon_plan.scores precedent). The module is descriptive (observations, not verdicts): it reports movement, it never prescribes a voice change and carries no Must/Should/Could severity (validator F4). The profile is local-only — make no external call and never transmit it (the no-telemetry mandate; validator W2 is an advisory hygiene check). Validated by scripts/author_fingerprint.py. See docs/author-voice-fingerprint.md.
title: APODICTIC author voice fingerprint (apodictic.voice_fingerprint.v1)

centroid_ref.$comment: Provenance: names the consumed audit output (e.g. 'results.baseline_comparison'). Presence + non-emptiness checked by F2 in author_fingerprint.py — not in `required` here because F2 owns it (the spec separates F1 schema from F2 provenance).

metrics.$comment: A flat map of scalar metric values (illustrative keys: mattr_z, mtld_z, burrows_to_author_centroid). The subset engine only types the container as object; author_fingerprint.py checks non-emptiness and that every value is a scalar (str/number/bool), not a nested object/array — the retcon_plan.scores precedent.
